rulings.Case Title: HUMANMADE, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT CORPORATION VS. 150 HOOPER, INC., ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 27, 2025, line 1. DEFENDANT PLACEMADE'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Continued to June 18, 2025, on the court's motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email addres. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. BONDI PARTNERS, PLLC, (DOMESTIC PROFESSIONAL LLC) ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 27, 2025, line 10. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT CAMERON TURNER'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Party Cameron C. Turner counsel Alana S. Yakolev and Bochner PLLC's motion to be relieved as counsel is DENIED. The departure of an attorney from a firm is not necessarily grounds for the firm to withdraw as counsel and the record does not establish grounds for a court-ordered withdrawal at this time. In any event, the court will not authorize a withdrawal less than six weeks before trial. Counsel must first obtain a trial continuance (which is not available from this department). Further if the client is served at a last known address, section 3(b) of the declaration must be completed. No later than 8:30 am on May 27, 2025, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above-text verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: DELORES MARTIN VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 27, 2025, line 2. PLAINTIFF DELORES MARTIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff Delores Martin's Motion for Summary Judgment or, in the Alternative, Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to file and serve the motion in compliance with the timing requirements of CCP 437c(a)(2) [81-day notice]. The court therefore cannot hear the motion. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 768 ["In sum, the statutory language and existing case law lead us to the conclusion a trial court does not have authority to shorten the minimum notice period for summary judgment hearings absent the express consent of the parties."].) Moreover, Plaintiff's motion is substantively defective because she presents no evidence regarding the amount of her alleged damages. A party cannot obtain summary judgment/adjudication and reserve the issue of damages for trial. (See Paramount Petroleum Corp. v. Superior Court (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 226, 241 ["A plaintiff can obtain summary adjudication of a cause of action only by proving 'each element of the cause of action entitling the party to judgment on that cause of action.' As damages are an element of a breach of contract cause of action [citation], a plaintiff cannot obtain judgment on a breach of contract cause of action in an amount of damages to be determined later."].) Per CCP 437c(a)(4), plaintiff may not bring another summary judgment motion. Defendant's request for sanction is denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. MYRNA LIM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 27, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANT MYRNA LIM'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant's motion to (1) vacate the default and default judgment entered on February 21, 2025, and (2) dismiss plaintiff's action is DENIED. On January 10, 2025, Plaintiff filed a proof of service with the court showing that registered process server Gustavo Moreira Machado Lino personally served defendant with a copy of the summons and complaint in this action. "The return of a process server registered pursuant to Chapter 16 (commencing with Section 22350) of Division 8 of the Business and Professions Code upon process or notice establishes a presumption, affecting the burden of producing evidence, of the facts stated in the return." (Evidence Code section 647.) Defendant has not presented any competent evidence to rebut the presumption of proper service. Defendant claims that the summons and complaint were merely posted on her door, but she fails to provide a declaration under penalty of perjury in compliance with CCP 2015.5 or any other competent evidence to support the assertion. Lastly, there is no basis to dismiss plaintiff's action. The court therefore DENIES the motion in its entirety. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA RAYGOZA VS. HAMMEL, GREEN AND ABRAHAMSON, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 27, 2025, line 6. DEFENDANTS WALDEK KACZMARSKI, DEEPA BALGI, HAMMEL, GREEN AND ABRAHAMSON, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations is denied. "Any party, within the time allowed to respond to a pleading may serve and file a notice of motion to strike the whole or any part thereof" (CCP 435.) "The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: [para] (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP 436.) "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP 452.) Defendants seek to strike Plaintiff's punitive damages allegations. "In an action for the breach of an obligation not arising from contract, where it is proven by clear and convincing evidence that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice, the plaintiff, in addition to the actual damages, may recover damages for the sake of example and by way of punishing the defendant." (Civil Code section 3294.) "The ultimate facts showing entitlement to such relief must be pled by a plaintiff." (Clauson v Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1253, 1255.) This is a pleading standard. (Grieves v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166.) "Not only must there be circumstances of oppression, fraud or malice, but facts must be alleged in the pleading to support such a claim." (Id.) "In passing on the correctness of a ruling on a motion to strike, judges read allegations of a pleading subject to a motion to strike as a whole, all parts in their context, and assume their truth." (Clauson, 67 Cal. App. 4th at 1255.) Plaintiff alleges sufficient ultimate facts to support the claim. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is denied. The news article profiling Ms. Balgi is not a proper subject for judicial notice under Evidence code section 452(h). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Court To Sign Stipulated Order Staying The Case Pending Resolution Of The Underlying Case
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 27, 2025, line 7. PLAINTIFFS INTERNATIONAL SWIMMING LEAGUE LTD., KONSTANTIN GRIGORISHIN'S Motion For Court To Sign Stipulated Order Staying The Case Pending Resolution Of The Underlying Case. The motion is GRANTED. Neil Goteiner's request to file an amicus brief is DENIED. This matter is stayed. The stay will dissolve once there is either a settlement or judgment entered in both of the two federal cases: (1) International Swimming League, Ltd. v. Federation Internationale de Natation, United States District Court, Case No. 3:18-cv-07394, (2) Shields v. Federation Internationale de Natation, United States District Court, Case No. 3:18-cv-07393.
